name: append_role_keywords
patch: When the question is about who appeared in, starred in, or shared cast between works, append short role keywords to each title: put the title in quotes and add one of {cast, starring, "cast list", credited} (e.g. "Title" cast). Keep each clause compact and keyword-like.
gradient_intent: Push queries toward pages that list cast/credits (film pages, actor pages, cast lists) so BM25 matches passages containing cast names and filmographies.
preservation_intent: Do not add full sentences or new actor names; preserve compact, keyword-like queries and avoid changing behavior on already-correct cast/film queries.

name: add_medium_disambiguators
patch: When a title may be ambiguous, append concise parenthetical disambiguators to the title: use one of (film), (TV series), (miniseries), (album), (band), (song) or include the year if present in the question as (YYYY film). Prefer short parentheticals rather than long descriptions.
gradient_intent: Steer queries to the correct media-specific pages (disambiguation to film/miniseries/album pages) so BM25 retrieves the right article type containing cast, release date, or other attributes.
preservation_intent: Keep queries terse and avoid adding extra content beyond the minimal disambiguator so correct current behaviors that rely on short queries are not disturbed.

name: attach_attribute_action_terms
patch: Append one or two compact attribute/action tokens relevant to the question: e.g. for teams/players add {"played for", "national team", "home stadium", "home matches"}; for albums add {"studio album", "release date", "released on"}; for soundtracks add {"soundtrack", "music by", "composer"}; for formation-date questions add {"formed", "formation year"}.
gradient_intent: Encourage queries to include the specific attribute language (played for, release date, soundtrack credits, formed) that matches passages with the factual answer so BM25 ranks relevant pages higher.
preservation_intent: Restrict additions to short attribute tokens only (no new assertions or candidate answers) to avoid altering behavior where current short-attribute queries already succeed.

name: quote_and_alternate_spellings
patch: Wrap exact titles/proper names in quotes and include common alternate spellings/diacritics or short ORvariants where relevant (e.g. "Félix Salinas" OR "Felix Salinas"). Use simple boolean OR only for compact alternates; do not inject guessed people/answers.
gradient_intent: Improve exact-match BM25 hits for named entities (handles accented vs non-accented forms and exact-title matches) so retrieval finds the correct entity page.
preservation_intent: Keep alternates minimal and limited to orthographic variants; avoid adding additional content that could change behavior on examples that are already correct.

name: place_alias_and_date_variants
patch: When the question mentions a place/county or historic entity, expand the hop2 query with compact alternate name variants and any date-range tokens from the question. Add short forms like "County" vs "Co." and swapped-name variants separated with OR (e.g. NameA OR NameB or NameA/NameB). Also include any explicit year-span found in the question as a separate keyword (e.g. 1848–1870). Keep the additions keyword-like and brief.
gradient_intent: move queries toward including alternate place-name spellings/abbreviations and relevant historical year ranges so BM25 retrieves pages that use different name variants
preservation_intent: do not add verbose sentences or full-sentence context; avoid changing queries that already correctly retrieve using exact canonical names

name: movement_aka_and_hashtag_expansion
patch: For protests/online-movement queries, add compact 'also-known-as' and hashtag variants. Append short OR clauses such as ""also known as" OR "also called" OR aka" and hashtag forms with/without punctuation (e.g. #word OR "word" and concatenated variants without spaces). Prefer quoted exact-phrase tokens and keep the whole query keyword-like.
gradient_intent: encourage retrieval of pages that refer to movements by alternate names or social-media hashtags so BM25 matches both formal and online labels
preservation_intent: avoid inserting long explanatory text or asserting answers; do not broaden to unrelated movements or add many extra topic terms

name: sports_name_variant_plus_suspension_terms
patch: When the question concerns player discipline/suspensions, expand player-name tokens into compact name variants (full name, surname-only, initial+surname e.g. "A. Surname") and add concise suspension vocabulary: suspend* OR suspended OR suspension OR PED OR "substance-abuse" OR "domestic violence". Combine with the year tokens from the question. Keep queries short and tokenized.
gradient_intent: push queries to include both name variants and discipline keywords so BM25 returns articles that mention suspensions even when names are abbreviated or context uses policy terms
preservation_intent: do not enumerate exhaustive player rosters or add unrelated team-history words; preserve compact, keyword-like style so correct examples that already match are unaffected

name: song_single_and_performer_aliases
patch: For music/song queries, add compact tokens like "(song)", "single", "lead single", and concise performer alias forms (stage name OR former band name) as short OR alternatives. Also include verbs/phrases like "performed by" or "sang" as keywords. Keep each added token brief and focused on matching title/performer mentions.
gradient_intent: nudge queries to match pages describing songs and performers (title-disambiguation pages, song pages, performer bios) so BM25 retrieves the correct song/artist pages
preservation_intent: do not add long biographical detail or explicit answer text; avoid adding extra genre/context words that could shift retrieval away from title/performer matches

name: use_or_quotes_for_variants
patch: When generating hop2 queries, prefer compact keyword sets: emit exact multiword phrases in quotes and join alternate spellings/names with OR (e.g. "Phrase A" OR "Phrase B" OR A_B). Keep tokens short and keyword-like; avoid full-sentence phrasing.
gradient_intent: push queries toward including quoted multiword phrases and OR'd name variants so BM25 matches title pages and alias pages
preservation_intent: do not alter queries that are already compact/keyword-like (they already use quotes/parentheses); only reformat phrasing to quoted OR lists

name: alias_probe_tokens_for_other_name_questions
patch: If the question asks for 'another name', 'also known as', 'alternate name', 'also called', or 'nickname', append short alias probes such as: aka OR "also known as" OR "also called" plus likely short variants/hashtags (no gold literals). Keep probes as keywords, not fluent sentences.
gradient_intent: nudge queries to explicitly search for alias/aka pages (captures pages like 'X also known as Y' or hashtag-origin pages)
preservation_intent: only apply when question explicitly requests an alternate/another name; leave other query types unchanged

name: episode_list_season_episode_tokens
patch: For TV episode/order questions, append compact episode-list tokens: include the series disambiguator (e.g. (TV series)), plus keywords like "List of [Series] episodes", "season N", "episode N", "S1E4", "episode title". Use quoted series name and numeric episode tokens; avoid verbose phrasing.
gradient_intent: encourage retrieval of episode list or episode-article pages by matching common list-page titles and season/episode tokens
preservation_intent: only trigger for episode/order questions; preserve queries that already target episode lists or specific episode pages

name: location_and_assignment_keyword_boosts
patch: When question asks about where something is located, based, or last stationed/assigned (keywords: located, based in, last stationed/assigned, headquartered, part of), append concise location/assignment probes: city OR county OR state OR country names, and short phrases like "based in" OR "headquartered" OR "last stationed". Keep tokens terse and avoid adding long explanatory text.
gradient_intent: pull queries toward pages that state location/headquarters/last station by matching concise location and assignment phrases
preservation_intent: only apply when the question requests location/assignment; do not add these probes for non-geographic intents
